section_miscategorizations.text: His last PSA was 0.66 and his last testosterone was greater than 1,500.
section_miscategorizations.reason: These are objective lab results rather than patient-reported symptoms or narrative history, so they more appropriately belong in Findings, though their presence in HPI is not highly misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's cholesterol and triglycerides were 265 milligrams per deciliter, which decreased to 145 milligrams per deciliter after starting testosterone.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This sentence primarily describes historical change over time in relation to treatment (a clinical course narrative) rather than a single current objective measurement, so it could fit better in HPI; however, as lab values, its placement in Findings is still understandable and not seriously misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: Red blood cell count has increased.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is an objective lab trend and is correctly placed in Findings.
